text: VO2max can be maintained at trained levels for at least 15 weeks when training frequency is reduced from 6 days/week to as low as 2 days/week, provided intensity and duration are preserved.
specific_value: 2 days/week minimum frequency for maintenance over 15 weeks

text: Ten weeks of endurance training at 6 days/week, 40 minutes/day produced a 25% increase in VO2max (bicycle testing) and 20% increase (treadmill testing).
Training intensity is a more critical factor than training frequency for maintaining aerobic fitness gains — high-intensity sessions at reduced frequency preserved VO2max fully.
More training volume is required to increase VO2max than to maintain it at a previously trained level when exercise intensity remains high.
Reducing training frequency by two-thirds (from 6 to 2 days/week) did not result in any measurable decline in VO2max over 15 weeks when session intensity and duration were unchanged.
specific_value: 66% frequency reduction with no VO2max loss
